commands.repair_reason: The remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer/security review of the approval auth boundary, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: No concrete security defect was found in the diff; the token remains scoped to loopback explicit URLs in the changed branch and remote explicit URL omission is tested. commands.review_summary:
The PR sends the approval runtime token for loopback explicit gateway URL overrides, keeps it off explicit remote overrides, and makes stale Discord approval button clicks show an ephemeral follow-up with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +19. Total +25 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main drops approvalRuntimeToken for any explicit gatewayUrl and suppresses followUp for not-found Discord approval resolution. I did not run a live Discord or gateway scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer/security review of the approval auth boundary, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix: repair local approval resolution
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep approval resolution on `operator.approvals`; do not grant `operator.write` for Discord approval decisions.
- Keep explicit `gatewayUrl` support, but attach `approvalRuntimeToken` when the resolved gateway URL is default/local loopback so local channel-managed approval clients can still get the internal approval-runtime marker.
- Omit `approvalRuntimeToken` for explicit remote gateway URLs.
- Show an ephemeral Discord follow-up when an approval button is clicked after the request is already resolved or expired.

## Scope split
- Kept in this PR: local/loopback approval-runtime token handling for `gatewayUrl` overrides, plus stale Discord approval-button UX.
- Split out: `approval.routeNotice.send` and approval route-notice gateway authorization.
- Split out: shared `exec-approvals.json` socket token support for Docker/CLI/separate-process approval clients sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Local explicit gateway URLs no longer suppress the process-local `approvalRuntimeToken`, while explicit remote gateway URLs still do not receive it. Stale/already-resolved Discord approval button clicks now produce a visible ephemeral follow-up instead of looking dead.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout for PR head `083c3d4c60`, Node `v24.14.0`, isolated worktree at `openclaw-pr-86771-split`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused approval client and Discord approval monitor Vitest targets with the repo Node wrapper, checked formatting on the touched files, and checked the staged diff for whitespace errors.

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts --reporter=dot

RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/jason/workspace/openclaw-pr-86771-split

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  26 passed (26)
Duration  1.99s

$ PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ git diff --check --cached
(no output)
```

Observed result after fix: The approval client contract now covers both explicit loopback and explicit remote `gatewayUrl` behavior: loopback keeps `approvalRuntimeToken`, remote omits it. The Discord button handler now acknowledges stale approval clicks and sends the ephemeral message `That approval request is no longer pending. It may have expired or already been resolved.`

What was not tested: No live Discord UI click or live Docker gateway was run in this split PR. Native approval route notices and separate-process shared socket-token support are intentionally out of scope for follow-up PRs.

## Tests and validation
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.test.ts`
- `git diff --check --cached`

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: approval-runtime authentication for explicit local gateway URLs.

Mitigation: the token is still process-local; it is only attached for default or loopback-resolved gateway URLs, and explicit remote gateway URLs continue to omit it.

## Current review state
Next action: review the narrowed approval-resolution diff and follow-up PRs for the split-out route-notice and shared-token changes.
